name: Almaty (Kazakhstan)

colors.symbolism: the sky over the Trans-Ili Alatau and the heritage of the Turkic peoples
the edge bands and the snowy peaks of the arms
the narrow bands framing the field

description: A light-blue field with narrow red-and-white edge bands, charged centrally with the golden coat of arms of Almaty — featuring the snowy Tian Shan range, a snow leopard and an apple, the city's namesake.
history: Almaty is the largest city in Kazakhstan and was the country's capital until 1997, when the seat of government was moved to Astana. The city has the status of a city of republican significance, administered separately from Almaty Region. The flag and coat of arms reflect both the surrounding Trans-Ili Alatau mountains and the apple from which the city's name derives (Kazakh almaty / alma = apple).

features.symbolism: the Trans-Ili Alatau mountains, a snow leopard and an apple — the city's namesake
